title: batch-bug-shepherd subagent return shapes
description: Five return shapes, one per wave (triage / strategic-alignment / shepherd / completion / conflict-resolution). Schema-validate every subagent return. On malformed, re-spawn ONCE; on second malformed, mark the row UNCLEAR (triage / shepherd / completion / conflict-resolution) or route as aligned with gate-failed-open annotation (strategic-alignment) and continue.

repro_steps.description: Smallest possible reproduction; required when verdict=LEGIT.

evidence.description: Command output, file paths, or commit refs that justify the verdict.

cited_principle.description: Verbatim section heading OR short sentence from PRINCIPLES.md that fires (for demote verdicts) or grounds the aligned verdict. On gate-fail-open infrastructure failures, the literal string 'gate-failed-open: <reason>'. NEVER empty; the gate fails OPEN to 'aligned' if no principle is citable, rather than demoting under infrastructure failure (truth #3 OUTPUT IS PROBABILISTIC + truth #4 HALLUCINATION IS INHERENT).

rationale.description: 1-3 sentences explaining the verdict in CEO voice. References the cited principle by name.

reservations.description: Required when verdict=aligned-with-reservations. Each entry is one specific concern downstream phases (panel / completion) must surface in their review prose. Omitted for other verdicts.

verdict.description: ready-to-merge iff blocking_followups is empty; otherwise needs-author-changes (or reject for fundamental design pushback). recommended_followups MAY be non-empty in any verdict and feeds the completion fan-out under the fold-in invariant.

blocking_followups.description: Severity-blocking findings. The completion subagent MUST resolve each entry before the PR is considered ready. Empty => verdict is ready-to-merge.

recommended_followups.description: Non-blocking but substantive findings the panel surfaced. Under the fold-in invariant (see SKILL.md Architecture invariants) the completion subagent classifies each as FOLD (cheap + on-path; lands in this PR) or DEFER (separable; opens a tracking issue).

source_persona.description: Which panelist surfaced this item (e.g. python-architect, supply-chain-security-expert, devx-ux-expert). The completion subagent uses this to pick the right panelist persona to consult when implementing the fix.

fold_hint.description: Optional hint from the panel about fold-vs-defer classification. Authoritative classification still happens in the completion subagent against the criteria in completion-prompt.md; this is advisory only.

ci_evidence.description: CI run URL or `gh pr checks` summary; required when status=ready-to-merge.

lint_evidence.description: Exit code and silent-output confirmation for the canonical ruff pair; required when status=ready-to-merge.

mutation_break_evidence.description: One entry per regression-trap test added: the test name, the guard deleted, and confirmation that the test failed without the guard.

folded_followups.description: Recommended items the completion subagent landed in THIS PR. Each entry cites the panel id and a one-line summary of what was implemented.

deferred_followups.description: Recommended items the completion subagent classified as separable and filed as tracking issues. tracking_issue is the new issue number returned by gh issue create.

rationale.description: One-sentence reason this item is separable rather than fold-in (e.g. cross-cutting refactor, new feature, broad doc restructure).

blocker.description: Required when status=blocked. One-paragraph human-readable explanation.

mergeStateStatus_pre.description: The mergeStateStatus value observed by Phase 5a probe BEFORE any rebase. Captured via `gh pr view --json mergeStateStatus`, never asserted from recall.

mergeStateStatus_post.description: The mergeStateStatus value observed by Phase 5c re-probe AFTER push. Required when status=resolved; must be one of CLEAN / UNSTABLE / HAS_HOOKS (anything else means the rebase did not in fact resolve).

conflicting_paths.description: Paths reported by `git status` during the rebase. Empty array when status=requires-author-action (no rebase attempted) is legal.

rebase_evidence.description: Brief human-readable narrative of the rebase: base SHA, head SHA pre-rebase, head SHA post-rebase, conflicts resolved, fallback used (merge-of-main) if any. Required when status=resolved.

push_command.description: The exact `git push ...` command used. MUST use --force-with-lease (never bare --force). The pattern rejects any --force not immediately followed by -with-lease. Required when status=resolved.

rebase_touched_regression_test.description: True if the rebase introduced changes to a test added during fold-in (regression-trap test). Drives whether mutation_break_evidence is required.

mutation_break_evidence.description: Required when rebase_touched_regression_test=true. Same shape as completion_return.mutation_break_evidence: re-run the gate post-rebase.

lint_evidence.description: Exit code and silent-output confirmation for the canonical ruff pair post-rebase. Required when status=resolved.

comment_url.description: URL of the SECOND-and-final PR comment (resolution-confirmation). Two-comment-per-PR cap honored: completion + resolution = 2.

fork_url.description: Required when status=requires-author-action: the contributor fork URL the orchestrator could not push to (maintainerCanModify=false).

maintainerCanModify.description: Required when status=requires-author-action: the boolean evidence that pushing was blocked.

blocker.description: Required when status in {requires-human-judgment, resolution-failed}. One-paragraph human-readable explanation of why the rebase was irrecoverable or what failed.
